steps.content: Leaderboards show player statistics and rankings. They're great for competitive games and tracking progress.

steps.instruction: Create a leaderboard with coins and level stats:
steps.starterCode:
-- Create a leaderstats folder
-- Add coins and level stats
-- Set initial values
steps.solution:
local leaderstats = Instance.new("Folder")
leaderstats.Name = "leaderstats"
leaderstats.Parent = game.Players.LocalPlayer

local coins = Instance.new("IntValue")
coins.Name = "Coins"
coins.Value = 0
coins.Parent = leaderstats

local level = Instance.new("IntValue")
level.Name = "Level"
level.Value = 1
level.Parent = leaderstats

print("Leaderboard created!")
steps.hint: Use Instance.new("IntValue") to create stat values

steps.instruction: Create a function to update the leaderboard:
steps.starterCode:
-- Create a function to update leaderboard
-- It should add coins and level up the player
-- Print confirmation
steps.solution:
local function updateLeaderboard()
  coins.Value = coins.Value + 10
  if coins.Value >= 100 then
    level.Value = level.Value + 1
    coins.Value = 0
    print("Level up! New level: " .. level.Value)
  else
    print("Coins: " .. coins.Value)
  end
end

updateLeaderboard()
steps.hint: Check if coins >= 100 to trigger a level up
